title: Update compiler flags via `--compiler`
body:
Thanks for taking the time to report this.

**What would you like added/supported?**

Currently Verilator supports the `--compiler` flag to indicate a compiler preference.
However, this option affects only the C++ code style as in V3Options.cpp without changing the compiler flags which seem to be configured at Verilator compile time.
This would cause compiling errors because some flags of clang++ are not accepted by g++, and the vice versa.
For example, I'm using g++ to build a design with `--timing`, with the Verilator configured by default using clang++.
If I override the make scripts with `CC=gcc CXX=g++ LINK=g++`, it returns errors as follows.
```
g++-10: error: unrecognized command-line option ‘-Qunused-arguments’
g++-10: error: unrecognized command-line option ‘-fbracket-depth=4096’
g++-10: error: unrecognized command-line option ‘-fbracket-depth=4096’
g++-10: error: unrecognized command-line option ‘-fcoroutines-ts’; did you mean ‘-fcoroutines’?
```

Actually I don't know the workaround except for configuring the Verilator with g++.

Can you attach an example that runs on other simulators?  (Must be openly licensed, ideally in test_regress format.)
N/A

**May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?**
Sure. If you could provide some help on how this could be fixed, I'm glad to implement it.
code_blocks:
g++-10: error: unrecognized command-line option ‘-Qunused-arguments’
g++-10: error: unrecognized command-line option ‘-fbracket-depth=4096’
g++-10: error: unrecognized command-line option ‘-fbracket-depth=4096’
g++-10: error: unrecognized command-line option ‘-fcoroutines-ts’; did you mean ‘-fcoroutines’?
